user_query:
I need a clean QTO and cost estimate from our BIM exports. Please read all four files under input/:
- input/design_elements.csv
- input/asbuilt_elements.csv
- input/unit_prices.csv
- input/waste_factors.json

Then produce exactly three deliverables under output/ with the following structure and rules:

1) QTO (as-built, by category with waste applied)
- Compute Net_Volume as the sum of as-built volumes per Category.
- Count is the number of as-built elements per Category.
- Apply the waste factor per Material from input/waste_factors.json at the element level, then aggregate to Category to compute Gross_Volume and Waste_Volume (Gross_Volume − Net_Volume).
- Round Net_Volume, Gross_Volume, and Waste_Volume to 2 decimals.
- Sort rows by Category ascending.
- Save to output/qto_asbuilt_by_category.csv with columns exactly: Category, Count, Net_Volume, Gross_Volume, Waste_Volume.

2) Cost estimate (as-built gross volume × unit price)
- For each Category, take Gross_Volume from the QTO above.
- Join with input/unit_prices.csv on Category to get Unit_Price (assume price is per m³).
- Compute Total_Cost = Gross_Volume × Unit_Price, rounded to 2 decimals.
- Compute Cost_Pct = (Total_Cost / sum of all Total_Cost) × 100, rounded to 1 decimal.
- Do not include a grand total row; include one row per Category only.
- Sort by Category ascending.
- Save to output/cost_estimate.csv with columns exactly: Category, Gross_Volume, Unit_Price, Total_Cost, Cost_Pct.

3) Design vs As-Built comparison (by category)
- For each Category, compute Design_Volume (sum of design volumes) and AsBuilt_Volume (sum of as-built volumes).
- Difference = AsBuilt_Volume − Design_Volume, rounded to 2 decimals.
- Variance_Pct = ((AsBuilt_Volume − Design_Volume) / Design_Volume) × 100, rounded to 1 decimal (treat 0/0 as 0.0 if encountered).
- Include categories present in the input files and sort by Category ascending.
- Save as a JSON array to output/comparison_by_category.json with objects having keys exactly: Category, Design_Volume, AsBuilt_Volume, Difference, Variance_Pct. Round volumes to 2 decimals and Variance_Pct to 1 decimal.

General requirements:
- Validate required columns exist in the inputs and fail clearly if they do not.
- Use only the data in input/.
- Do not write any other files besides the three specified outputs.
- Use consistent rounding as specified and ensure column names and ordering match exactly.
